memberdef.name: ADDRESS_1_OFFSET
memberdef.initializer: 1 /* MSB byte of address to read or write */

memberdef.name: ADDRESS_2_OFFSET
memberdef.initializer: 2 /* Middle byte of address to read or write */

memberdef.name: ADDRESS_3_OFFSET
memberdef.initializer: 3 /* LSB byte of address to read or write */

memberdef.name: DUMMY_SIZE
memberdef.initializer: 1 /* Number of dummy bytes for fast read */

memberdef.name: RD_ID_SIZE
memberdef.initializer: 4 /* Read ID command + 3 bytes ID response */

briefdescription.para: This function is the handler which performs processing for the SPI driver.

detaileddescription.para: It is called from an interrupt context such that the amount of processing performed should be minimized. It is called when a transfer of SPI data completes or an error occurs.
This handler provides an example of how to handle SPI interrupts but is application specific.

parameterdescription.para: is a reference passed to the handler.
is the status of the SPI .
is the number of bytes transferred.

memberdef.argsstring: (XSpiPs *SpiPtr, u32 Address, u32 ByteCount, u8 Command)
memberdef.name: FlashWrite

memberdef.argsstring: (XSpiPs *SpiPtr, u32 Address, u32 ByteCount, u8 Command)
memberdef.name: FlashRead

briefdescription.para: The purpose of this function is to illustrate how to use the XSpiPs device driver in interrupt mode.

detaileddescription.para: This function writes and reads data from a serial flash.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the SPI driver instance to use.
contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para:
- In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.
This function calls other functions which contain loops that may be infinite if interrupts are not working such that it may not return. If the device slave select is not correct and the device is not responding on bus it will read a status of 0xFF for the status register as the bus is pulled up.

briefdescription.para: Main function to call the SPI Flash example.
This file contains a design example using the SPI driver (XSpiPs) in interrupt mode with a serial flash device.

detaileddescription.para: This examples performs transfers in Manual start mode using interrupts. The hardware which this example runs on, must have a serial flash for it to run. This example has been tested with SST25W080.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 sg 1/30/13 First release ms 04/05/17 Modified Comment lines in functions to recognize it as documentation block for doxygen generation. 3.2 nsk 03/26/19 Add support for versal 3.9 sb 07/05/23 Added support for system device-tree flow. 3.13 sb 09/09/25 Fix flash status register check in erase function 3.14 vlt 12/18/25 Update Doxygen comments to include SDT flow details.
